records.ety: [L. informis; pref. in- not + forma form, shape: cf. F. informe]

defs: Without regular form; shapeless; ugly; deformed.

records.ety: [OE. enformen, OF. enformer, F. informer. L. informare; pref. in- in + formare to form, share, fr. forma form. See Form.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Informed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Informing.]

defs: To give form or share to; to give vital or organizing power to; to give life to; to imbue and actuate with vitality; to animate; to mold; to figure; to fashion.
quotes: Let others better mold the running mass Of metals, and inform the breathing brass.
Breathes in our soul, informs our mortal part.

defs: To communicate knowledge to; to make known to; to acquaint; to advise; to instruct; to tell; to notify; to enlighten; -- usually followed by of.
quotes: For he would learn their business secretly, And then inform his master hastily.
I am informed thoroughly of the cause.

defs: To communicate a knowledge of facts to, by way of accusation; to warn against anybody.
quotes: Tertullus . . . informed the governor against Paul.

defs: To take form; to become visible or manifest; to appear.
quotes: It is the bloody business which informs Thus to mine eyes.

defs: To give intelligence or information; to tell.
quotes: He might either teach in the same manner, or inform how he had been taught.
